name: Eli Stowers
headline: Mackey-winning rookie waits behind Goedert in Philly
body:
Philadelphia spent the 54th overall pick on Stowers, a 6-foot-3, 239-pound Vanderbilt product who captured the 2025 John Mackey Award after a 62-catch, 769-yard, four-touchdown season. The athletic profile is loud: a 4.51 forty and a 45.5-inch vertical hint at a long-term mismatch weapon. The problem for redraft purposes is the depth chart. Dallas Goedert still owns the starting job, and Stowers projects as the No. 2, capping his year-one ceiling.

That reality shows up in his pricing. He carries a #194 overall ADP, TE29, with expert ranks landing around TE34, while dynasty managers value him far higher at TE10 in early second-round rookie territory. The modest projection of roughly 23 catches, 232 yards and 1.5 scores reflects a rotational rookie role. Questions about whether his frame holds up in-line could pinch snaps further. Stash him and bet on the breakout once Philadelphia's tight end depth chart opens up.
